title: reseda alba subsp. paui
intro: Reseda alba subsp. paui, also known as White Mignonette, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of alkaloids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Reseda alba subsp. paui include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, and it has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Reseda alba subsp. paui can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it can also be used topically in the form of creams and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
